meta.title: MFA Documentary Film Scoring Model
meta.purpose: Evidence-weighted documentary MFA fit and sustainability analysis.
notes: Pamunkey documentary is tagged exploratory/non-core and should not drive portfolio fit scoring.
Static HTML cannot autonomously scrape the live web. Web-linked updating requires API/serverless/Sheets/backend integration.

v4 adds visualization layers: radar charts, topology map, uncertainty bars, timeline graphs, energy budget, relationship network, identity safety heatmap, portfolio resonance, admissions quadrant, scenario cascade, and HRC MEI city scores where verified.
v5 introduces a master ontology for long-term creative-life simulation architecture.
v6 activates every future system except 16mm reverence intensity as dashboard-accessible, school-specific seeded modules.
v7 integrates public Reddit/FilmSchool.org testimony into school records and system-module confidence estimates.
v8 adds alumni/current-student outcomes and trajectory-pattern evidence for each school.

v9 adds forecasting engine layers: evidence hierarchy, documentary ontology mapping, advisor archetypes, ordinary Tuesday realism, anti-delusion checks, stress tests, reflection schemas, and comparative future biographies.
v10 adds calibration, adversarial review, archetype comparison, film-analysis schema, language analysis, negative-case framework, social-network topology, and identity-preservation forecasting.
v11 adds advanced research systems and major visual/UI redesign scaffolding.
v12 makes operational modules work in-browser with editable calculators, score outputs, and JSON export.
v13-static removes backend/live-ingestion assumptions and reframes the project as a static inference model using local JSON, evidence quality, confidence, and manual updates.
v14 adds in-page JSON editing, validation, download/save controls, and a redesigned interface under the name The Documentary Futures Atlas.
v15 rebrands the system as ATLAS.
v16 fixes file:// loading by embedding data fallback while preserving data.json override when served over HTTP.
v18 optimizes desktop/mobile, adds logout, and adds additional static visualization modes.
v19 rebuilds ATLAS as a coherent unified architecture.
v20 removes old-version artifacts, collapses overlapping modules, and normalizes ATLAS into a cleaner decision system.
v21 removes excess visual clutter, adds collapsible icon navigation, page-specific explanations, corner JSON editor, city photo cards, and expanded visualization options.
v22 adds visual-first location section, bundled local city cards, brand color coding, clickable school names, circular score rings, and local resource links.

v23 adds comparison mode, decision presets, evidence-state visuals, source trace hooks, mentorship dependency, future-self cards, environmental rhythm, daily-cost friction, and documentary ecosystem mapping.

backend_policy.data_update_method: manual JSON replacement or in-browser JSON edit/export
backend_policy.modeling_position: Use verified facts where available; make explicit, labeled inferences where direct data is unavailable; never present inferred values as verified facts.

schools.ordinary_tuesday: Likely structured, lower-friction, centered around class/editing/story meetings, with enough quiet to recover. Main stressors: funding uncertainty and NC politics.

comparative_future_biography.5_year: Harper likely emerges as a grounded documentary storyteller with stronger teaching identity and a sustainable working rhythm.
comparative_future_biography.10_year: Likely building a teaching/documentary practice with regional or institutional roots, possibly mentoring undergraduates and making relationship-centered films.
comparative_future_biography.20_year: Most plausible path toward becoming the kind of professor-filmmaker Harper imagines: relational, sustainable, community-oriented.
comparative_future_biography.possible_regret: May wonder whether the formal/cinematic ceiling could have been higher elsewhere.

anti_delusion_checks: Do not turn Wake into destiny; keep verifying funding, actual faculty availability, and artistic ceiling.
Chris warmth is strong evidence, but not a guarantee of institutional perfection.
NC politics cannot be emotionally handwaved.

outcome_taxonomy.interpretation_note: Outcomes are not prestige scores. They indicate what kind of professional/artistic life each program appears to normalize.

official_curriculum.description: Published curriculum, degree requirements, official course lists.

official_funding.description: Official funding/financial aid pages or written program confirmation.

direct_faculty_behavior.description: Observed email/call tone, specificity, follow-through, responsiveness.

direct_student_testimony.description: Known/current student or alum testimony, especially specific examples.

public_student_anecdote.description: Reddit/FilmSchool.org/RMP public posts; useful but noisy.

official_marketing.description: Program rhetoric not tied to concrete requirements/outcomes.

inference_from_context.description: Reasoned extrapolation from city, institutional culture, or adjacent evidence.

calibration_engine.purpose: Compare predicted outcomes against later lived outcomes and update confidence accordingly.

adversarial_review_engine.purpose: Actively challenge dominant conclusions and identify model bias.
core_questions: What evidence would overturn the current Wake hypothesis?
What if Stanford pressure concerns are projection?
What if DePaul looseness is protective rather than dangerous?
What if prestige anxiety is overweighted?
What if warmth is being mistaken for rigor?
What if rigor is being mistaken for unsustainability?

advanced_research_layers.manual_evidence_pipeline_note: No backend/live scraping. These are manual research checklists for future updates.

decision_pressure_detector.description: Flags spiraling, overresearch, prestige fixation, catastrophizing, and evidence saturation.

application_material_alignment.description: Manual scoring tool for SOP/portfolio/email drafts by school.

thesis_simulator.description: Estimates thesis feasibility from access, scope, support, funding, health, and ethics.

collaborator_compatibility.description: Tracks collaborator types that stabilize or destabilize Harper.

rejection_recovery_model.description: Plans emotional and strategic recovery if rejected from a program.

attraction_decomposition.description: Separates attraction drivers so prestige/fantasy do not masquerade as fit.

stop_researching_detector.description: Estimates whether further research is useful or avoidance.

static_update_workflow.step_1: Collect new evidence manually: faculty email, program page, student testimony, alumni example, visit log, or public post.
static_update_workflow.step_3: Update affected subvariables and system modules in data.json.
static_update_workflow.step_4: Record inference notes separately from verified facts.
static_update_workflow.step_5: Export or replace data.json alongside index.html.
static_update_workflow.step_6: Use confidence and pending flags to prevent false certainty.

direct_fact.definition: Explicitly stated by official source, direct email, or named person.

corroborated_pattern.definition: Multiple independent pieces of evidence point in same direction.
corroborated_pattern.example: Wake appears filmmaker-educator oriented across curriculum, student bios, and faculty rhetoric.

structured_inference.definition: Reasoned estimate from related facts, not directly observed.
structured_inference.example: Stanford may increase prestige pressure because of institutional context.

placeholder_prior.definition: Default estimate used until evidence appears.

visualization_modules.category_heatmap: Compare category strengths across all schools.
visualization_modules.risk_dashboard: View regret, failure-mode, and identity-preservation risk together.
visualization_modules.method_scorecard: Show score, confidence, evidence/inference policy, and pending areas in one diagnostic view.

canonical_model.primary_identity: structured evidence-weighted documentary MFA fit and sustainability analysis system
core_principles: Prefer decision-useful synthesis over speculative sprawl.
Use inferences when helpful, but label them.
